reviews.review_msg: Limiting for a strategy course, little room for multiple perspectives. The professor was often dismissive and rude, sometimes laughing at answers that didn't match her views, which discouraged participation. She was frequently late but enforced attendance, and mainly read off slides. Class was a waste of money and time due to poor teaching.
This is simply chaotic teaching. She knows nothing herself. Why is this person still continuing to be a professor? Her qualifications need to be reviewed.

She is always 10–15 minutes late but calls out students who are only a few minutes late. Participation grading feels unfair and makes faces to students asking questions questions, making the class uncomfortable. She also has a strong accent that makes her hard to understand. I would not take her again. Hope she learns how to teach properly.

Material's easy and exams r all mcq, just watch out for questions on details of random ads shown. Hard to understand, she rambles too much. Honestly confused how she is a SFU prof? Learned nothing from attending & have been avoiding her classes since taking. However it's Impressive how she balances her jacket on her shoulders for the entire class

She acts as if her opinions are always right and everyone else's are wrong. She seems to believe that she's the smartest person in the universe.. Doesn't know how to talk to people, extremely arrogant.

She was definitely the worst instructor I had at Beedie. I am not sure why she chose to teach since she doesn't seem to connect with students at all. She would ask us to share our thoughts, and then acted very condescending when you'd share something she doesn't agree with. Very arrogant.
DONOT TAKE HER.....

SFU is facing such a shortage of professors and instructors that they've had to hire someone like this to teach a class. It's very hard to understand her because of her accent and the way she speaks. On top of that, she is very arrogant and doesn't seem to have teaching skills at all. I don't know what I'm supposed to learn from her..

This professor is incompetent. She can't teach partly because she mumbles when she talks so you can't even hear the words she's saying and she's always vague when you ask her questions. She always reads of the slides and her feedback is not helpful. You could not attend class and just study on your own, that was more effective.
Very bad instructor, she just read the slides and without any useful explanations, just answered with "yes yes yes"? How can students understand the concepts?
Doesn't have sufficient English skills to teach. Very arrogant, has poor listening and speaking skills, and doesn't know how to speak to people respectfully.

Yuri is one of the worst professors I've had at SFU. Her slides are straight from the textbook with no added insight, so attending lectures feels pointless. She mumbles through explanations, and if you ask for clarification, she just stares at you. Many case studies were irrelevant, and her exams demand exact answers with no flexibility. Avoid!

Don't listen to any of the negative reviews here, Yuri is an amazing professor. Her lectures are great, connecting course concepts into real world examples. You'll do well if you study and participate. She is hilarious and easily has the best fashion sense out of all profs, some of you need to take some notes. 

She won't hold your hand, grow up.
Never take it with her. She does not respond to emails all semester, does not accept sick notes if you can't come to class. JUST AVOID HER!
Just don't take it with her

Avoid at all cost. What everyone is saying here is true. She mumbles too much in a super quiet voice and doesn't teach anything! Class asked her what chapters to study for the exam and she just laughed at us and told us she wont be telling us??? Odd way of teaching and doesn't provide any help when you reach out.

Avoid avoid avoid. She makes lecture a complete waste of time but mandatory for participation marks. She is passive aggressive and will make faces at you if you don't say exactly what she's thinking during discussion. She reads her slides off in 2x speed and never actually provides valuable information for learning. Worst prof ever!
She mumbles when talking in her lectures, and when you ask her to repeat herself, she makes a weird face and talks even quieter. Overall a really bad prof but her exams weren't that hard.
dont take a class with her i beg you, she is so terrible, avoid at all costs. the good reviews about her are prolly just her writing them. theres nothing good about this prof. dont take her class

Horrible prof. No structure at all. Changes things on a whim like how participation is graded. Gives long breaks then speedruns the slides like crazy. Good luck understanding anything. Re case studies, u r given 1 mark for each valid point brought up but she didn't tell us that before. Some of us focused on quality over quantity of idea n got low

Terrible experience. The lectures lacked any meaningful information and it often felt like she just read off the slides without explaining the material. The exams were poorly written—vague questions that didn't reflect what was covered in class. It felt like she didn't care about students or whether we understood the content. Avoid this professor.

DO NOT TAKE ANY CLASS WITH YURI. The worse prof at SFU. Mumbles & then makes faces when you ask her to repeat her self. During lectures she reads through the slides at warp speed. Has incredibly confusing grading criteria & doesn't explain it when you ask. Does not care about students and is unaccessible outside of class. Avoid her at all costs!
she doesn't care about students and she also has a poor listening skill. grading criteria is hilarious. outside the class, she is not approachable at all
so hard and unfair

The actual work may have been ok but she didnt rlly care abt her students much. SUPER disorganized during the school strike as during those 3 weeks she didn't communicate to us that classes are cancelled for us. she also announced our exam ONE day before class. Overall the workload and difficulty is fine.

Was a strike during the semester, but prof made zero efforts to communicate with the class. Professor cancelled midterm day before the exam and sent an email the next morning saying it was back on. DO NOT waste time sending prof an email, she will not respond. Read the textbook to succeed on exams. Be prepared to learn all material on your own.

Participate in class and make sure she knows your name. You will do well if you come prepared to class with answers to her questions. The participation questions are more or less the same every week so its easy to prepare and have answers ready. Exams are also quite straightforward. No final, just two midterms

Wouldn't trust the reviews completely. Yuri knows her stuff and has lots of experience which is great to learn from. The deliverables for the class are simple and she gives grading criteria. The exams are easy to pass if you have read the cases and studied the slides. Straightforward 400-level class

Take the bad reviews with a pinch of salt. I found Yuri's class easy to do well in if you participate. The exams are simple and off the slides, not cumulative and only midterms so you are free after your last presentation. Read the cases and participate, and you will do well. Its a good class and I would take it again.

the course was poorly structured, with no clear learning objectives or course outline. The course schedule was constantly changing, which made it difficult to plan and keep up with the course requirements. The course materials were often disorganized, and the instructions were unclear.
